All rights reserved. # OpenGrok - a wicked fast source browser [Permalink: OpenGrok - a wicked fast source browser](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#opengrok---a-wicked-fast-source-browser) [![GitHub actions build](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/actions/workflows/build.yml/badge.svg?branch=master)](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/actions)[![Coverage status](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/7f60d8db20243620c267f06f932d76e80d9894fa64405465c0c6be1d2366c63d/68747470733a2f2f736f6e6172636c6f75642e696f2f6170692f70726f6a6563745f6261646765732f6d6561737572653f70726f6a6563743d6f72672e6f70656e67726f6b2533416f70656e67726f6b2d746f70266d65747269633d636f766572616765)](https://sonarcloud.io/summary/new_code?id=org.opengrok%3Aopengrok-top)[![SonarQube status](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/00bb96318100fc2c87499b4d96c0ed9d71ebf36810f3397abf82936bb2e11c17/68747470733a2f2f736f6e6172636c6f75642e696f2f6170692f70726f6a6563745f6261646765732f6d6561737572653f70726f6a6563743d6f72672e6f70656e67726f6b2533416f70656e67726f6b2d746f70266d65747269633d616c6572745f737461747573)](https://sonarcloud.io/dashboard?id=org.opengrok%3Aopengrok-top)[![License](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/069ea630dd113b99d73786a9b35beab618a938f9d9c9c3379f11436f5fcd6372/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f4c6963656e73652d4344444c253230312e302d626c75652e737667)](https://opensource.org/licenses/CDDL-1.0) - [OpenGrok - a wicked fast source browser](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#opengrok---a-wicked-fast-source-browser) - [1\. Introduction](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#1-introduction) - [2\. OpenGrok install and setup](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#2-Installation) - [3\. Information for developers](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#3-information-for-developers) - [4\. Authors](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#4-authors) - [5\. Contact us](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#5-contact-us) - [6\. Run as container](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#6-run-as-container) ## 1\. Introduction [Permalink: 1. Introduction](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#1-introduction) OpenGrok is a fast and usable source code search and cross reference engine, written in Java. It helps you search, cross-reference and navigate your source tree. It can understand various program file formats and version control histories of many source code management systems. Official page of the project is on: [https://oracle.github.io/opengrok/](https://oracle.github.io/opengrok/) ## 2\. Installation [Permalink: 2. Installation](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#2-installation) See [https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/wiki/How-to-setup-OpenGrok](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/wiki/How-to-setup-OpenGrok) ### 2\. 1. Updating [Permalink: 2. 1. Updating](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#2-1-updating) OpenGrok uses [semantic versioning](https://semver.org/) and the version components further indicate more details about updating to newer version. The version scheme is _major.minor.micro_ and change in any component is interpreted as: - major - major backwards incompatible update, will require full reindex as well as configuration changes - minor - full clean reindex of your repositories is needed (e. g. index format has changed) - micro - redeploy web application Generally it is possible to go backward only within the micro version. ## 3\. Information for developers [Permalink: 3. Information for developers](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#3-information-for-developers) See [https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/wiki/Developer-intro](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/wiki/Developer-intro) and [https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/wiki/Developers](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/wiki/Developers) ## 4\. Authors [Permalink: 4. Authors](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#4-authors) The project has been originally conceived in [Sun Microsystems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sun_Microsystems) by Chandan B.N. For full list of contributors see [https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/graphs/contributors](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/graphs/contributors) ## 5\. Contact us [Permalink: 5. Contact us](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#5-contact-us) Use the [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/discussions). ## 6\. Run as container [Permalink: 6. Run as container](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#6-run-as-container) You can run OpenGrok as a Docker container as described [here](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/blob/master/docker/README.md). ## Contributing [Permalink: Contributing](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok#contributing) This project welcomes contributions from the community. web.title: OpenGrok - Oracle Open Source - GitHub Pages
web.description: OpenGrok is a fast and usable source code search and cross reference engine. It helps you search, cross-reference and navigate your source tree.
web.markdown:
## {OpenGrok

OpenGrok is a fast and usable source code search and cross reference engine. It
helps you search, cross-reference and navigate your source tree. It
understands various program file formats and history from many Source Code
Management systems.
In other words it lets you _grok_ (profoundly understand)
source code and is developed in the open, hence the name OpenGrok. It is
written in Java.


Requirements:


- [Java](http://www.oracle.com/technetwork/java/) 17 or later

- A servlet container like [GlassFish](https://javaee.github.io/glassfish/)
or [Tomcat](http://tomcat.apache.org/) (10.x or later)
also running with Java version as specified above

- [Universal ctags](https://github.com/universal-ctags/ctags)

Wiki pages:


- [Features](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/wiki/Features)
- [Comparison with Similar Tools](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/wiki/Comparison-with-Similar-Tools)
- [Supported Revision Control Systems](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/wiki/Supported-Revision-Control-Systems)
- [Supported Languages and Formats](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/wiki/Supported-Languages-and-Formats)
- [How to build OpenGrok from source](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/wiki/How-to-build-OpenGrok-from-source)
- [How to setup OpenGrok](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/wiki/How-to-setup-OpenGrok)
- [Internals](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/wiki/Internals)
- [Story of OpenGrok](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/wiki/Story-of-OpenGrok)

Communication:


- [Discussions\\
(mailing lists, etc.)](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/wiki/Discussions)
- Bugs or feature requests should be reported via
[Issues](https://github.com/oracle/opengrok/issues).

- Join the OpenGrok users group on [LinkedIn](http://linkedin.com/).


License:


OpenGrok is an open source project and is distributed under
[CDDL](http://www.opensource.org/licenses/cddl1.php).
OpenGrok uses software libraries from [Apache Software Foundation](http://apache.org/).
This product includes software developed by
[CollabNet](http://www.collab.net/).


OpenGrok developers Idea enterprise licenses are supported by [Jetbrains](https://www.jetbrains.com/).

[IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) can be used to edit {OpenGrok sources.

[![](https://oracle.github.io/opengrok/images/logo-idea.svg)](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/)

Monitoring and profiling {OpenGrok is supported by [YourKit Java Profiler](https://www.yourkit.com/java/profiler/).
[![](https://oracle.github.io/opengrok/images/yklogo.png)](https://www.yourkit.com/)

# [OpenGrok: Searching through the Haiku code online](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/t/opengrok-searching-through-the-haiku-code-online/15822)

[Development](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/c/development/5)

You have selected **0** posts.

[select all](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/t/opengrok-searching-through-the-haiku-code-online/15822)

2 / 22


Oct 2024

## post by nipos on Oct 27, 2024

[![](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/user_avatar/discuss.haiku-os.org/nipos/48/8966_2.png)](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/u/nipos)

[nipos](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/u/nipos)

[Oct 2024](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/t/opengrok-searching-through-the-haiku-code-online/15822 "Post date")

Sometimes it can be helpful to quickly search specific functions or constants in a large codebase.

Say I see `B_DARKEN_1_TINT` somewhere and want to see where it’s defined,so I can pick the right inverse tint for dark mode.

I think that was my first time I stumpled upon OpenGrok,which I found really helpful.

Unfortunately the longstanding OpenGrok instance on [https://xref.landonf.org](https://xref.landonf.org/) that’s mentioned in the developer docs has been offline for quite some weeks now and I’m not sure if it will ever come back.

As alternative, [https://grok.dragonflybsd.org](https://grok.dragonflybsd.org/) was recommended to me in the chat,which seems to work currently but also had multiple days long outages when I tried to work with it,leading me to do a slow local search in my cloned repository.

To fill the gap and offer a way to efficiently search the Haiku source again,I’ve put up my own OpenGrok instance dedicated to Haiku-related projects.

![:arrow_right:](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/images/emoji/twitter/arrow_right.png?v=12)**[https://grok.nikisoft.one](https://grok.nikisoft.one/)**

It currently has the following repositories:

- [https://review.haiku-os.org/haiku](https://review.haiku-os.org/haiku)
- [GitHub - haiku/haikuwebkit: Official Haiku fork of the WebKit repository](https://github.com/haiku/haikuwebkit)
- [GitHub - haiku/website: The Haiku website. (Pull requests are accepted; please file issues at https://dev.haiku-os.org).](https://github.com/haiku/website)
- [https://review.haiku-os.org/userguide](https://review.haiku-os.org/userguide)
- [GitHub - haikuports/haikuports: Software ports for the Haiku operating system.](https://github.com/haikuports/haikuports)
- [GitHub - haikuports/haikuporter: The tool that builds HaikuPorts recipes.](https://github.com/haikuports/haikuporter)
- [GitHub - haiku/haikudepotserver: Haiku Depot Server](https://github.com/haiku/haikudepotserver)
- [GitHub - haiku/haiku-format-bot: Tool that applies haiku-format to Gerrit changes](https://github.com/haiku/haiku-format-bot)
- [https://review.haiku-os.org/buildtools](https://review.haiku-os.org/buildtools)
- [GitHub - haiku/hpkgbouncer: A rust microservice to serve package repositories from public s3 buckets](https://github.com/haiku/hpkgbouncer)
- [https://review.haiku-os.org/jamfile-engine](https://review.haiku-os.org/jamfile-engine)

Maybe it will help you with your Haiku-related development ![:wink:](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/images/emoji/twitter/wink.png?v=12)

Feel free to suggest additional repositories that you need and let me know if anything doesn’t work as it should.

## post by nipos on Oct 27, 2024

[![](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/user_avatar/discuss.haiku-os.org/nipos/48/8966_2.png)](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/u/nipos)

[Oct 2024](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/t/opengrok-searching-through-the-haiku-code-online/15822/3 "Post date")

Yes,it has a REST API,see the API documentation here: [https://opengrok.docs.apiary.io/](https://opengrok.docs.apiary.io/)

An example to search for `B_PANEL_TEXT_COLOR` in the haiku repository: [https://grok.nikisoft.one/opengrok/api/v1/search?full=B\_PANEL\_TEXT\_COLOR&project=haiku](https://grok.nikisoft.one/opengrok/api/v1/search?full=B_PANEL_TEXT_COLOR&project=haiku)

## post by PulkoMandy on Oct 28, 2024

[![](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/user_avatar/discuss.haiku-os.org/pulkomandy/48/58_2.png)](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/u/pulkomandy)

[PulkoMandy](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/u/pulkomandy)
Developer


[Oct 2024](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/t/opengrok-searching-through-the-haiku-code-online/15822/13 "Post date")

I just learnt by reading this topic that I have one…

Unless someone hacked into my server and set it up there, I don’t think that instance exist. I experimented with Woboq, but I don’t regularly keep it up to date. Now that compile\_commands.json export is available in jam, it is considerably easier to use local tools like sourcetrail: [GitHub - CoatiSoftware/Sourcetrail: Sourcetrail - free and open-source interactive source explorer](https://github.com/CoatiSoftware/Sourcetrail?tab=readme-ov-file)

but I know the sourcecode well enough that usually I get around with a few git grep operations, and don’t really bother with more complicated tools.

## post by nipos on Oct 28, 2024

[![](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/user_avatar/discuss.haiku-os.org/nipos/48/8966_2.png)](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/u/nipos)

[nipos](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/u/nipos)

[![](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/user_avatar/discuss.haiku-os.org/nephele/24/8166_2.png)nephele](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/t/opengrok-searching-through-the-haiku-code-online/15822 "Load parent post")

[Oct 2024](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/t/opengrok-searching-through-the-haiku-code-online/15822/15 "Post date")

Dark mode should be available and somewhat working now.

I merged [Opengrok Dark Theme CSS · GitHub](https://gist.github.com/Codetector1374/6e4d234abeeeec349bc01ab0bc298598) into the default CSS file inside of a @media(prefers-color-scheme: dark) block and fixed up the most obvious white areas.

It’s not perfect,but should be good enough until a official dark mode theme from OpenGrok is available.

For switching branches,I don’t think that’s possible on OpenGrok as it heavily relies on the checked-out work directory on the server,which is set to the default branch for each repository.

9 days later

Yes I noticed linux there, but uboot is not hosted there or anywhere else on the internet. At least I could not find.

If you can consider hosting just uboot, that would be highly appreciated. uboot is a good reference for Haiku community as well.

## post by nipos on Nov 7, 2024

[![](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/user_avatar/discuss.haiku-os.org/nipos/48/8966_2.png)](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/u/nipos)

[nipos](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/u/nipos)

[Nov 2024](https://discuss.haiku-os.org/t/opengrok-searching-through-the-haiku-code-online/15822/19 "Post date")

I don’t really see a connection between Haiku and Uboot,but the Uboot repository isn’t that big and my server has enough free resources,so I decided to add it anyway.

I cloned the Uboot repository to the server.

It should get added to OpenGrok automatically when the next daily refresh task runs.

metadata.title: OpenGrok: Searching through the Haiku code online - Development - Haiku Community
metadata.og:description: Sometimes it can be helpful to quickly search specific functions or constants in a large codebase.  Say I see B_DARKEN_1_TINT somewhere and want to see where it’s defined,so I can pick the right inverse tint for dark mode.  I think that was my first time I stumpled upon OpenGrok,which I found really helpful.  Unfortunately the longstanding OpenGrok instance on https://xref.landonf.org that’s mentioned in the developer docs has been offline for quite some weeks now and I’m not sure if it will eve...
